author: Lisa Donovan
commentBody: This version of the cake is unfamiliar to this Southerner, but it's an excellent recipe.  The Swiss buttercream is far better than the usually over-unctuous American buttercream.  I still prefer a lighter frosting for coconut cake.  For those hesitant to use the sugar, choose another cake.  Coconut cake is traditionally very sweet, and it fades if it's not supported adequately by sugar.  That said, the 2 cups of sugar can be reduced by about 1/3 cup without doing real damage.

author: Lisa Donovan
commentBody: This is an excellent version of coconut cake, though it's unfamiliar to this Southerner.  The Swiss buttercream works far better than the heavy and overly-unctuous American buttercream.
readable_ai_guess: Liked recipe and prepared as written

author: Julia Moskin
recipeTitle: Coq au Vin Blanc Meatballs Recipe
commentBody: This is very tasty, but it has nothing at all to do with coq au vin.  Instead, it's a play on the basic French combinations of wine and cream, with the fabulous underflavors of mushroom and bacon.  Think more along the lines of Julia Child's wonderful recipe for poulet au porto.

author: Kenji López-Alt
recipeTitle: San Francisco-Style Vietnamese American Garlic Noodles Recipe
commentBody: Why would anyone eat "San Francisco style" Vietnamese food?  The largest Vietnamese population is 45 minutes away in San Jose.  And the Vietnamese culinary capital in this country is Houston.

author: Kenji López-Alt
commentBody: The tuna salad itself is good, though quite standard.  Putting chips inside the finished product is an affectation.  Plus, they quickly loose their crunch from the liquid ingredients.

author: Yotam Ottolenghi
recipeTitle: Grilled Asparagus With Miso and Olives Recipe
commentBody: The sauce seriously overpowers the flavor of the asparagus and is much too heavy for this delicate and wonderful vegetable.  The dill is optional, fortunately; dill does not complement asparagus at all.  Nothing is better than asparagus, butter, and a bit of salt.
readable_ai_guess: Didn't like recipe due to flavor

author: Priya Krishna
commentBody: I don't understand the enthusiasm for this cake.  The pudding mix (and the rather odd marshmallow base) gave it a soft, somewhat spongy, texture.  More importantly, the cake has almost no pistachio flavor.  You'll have to add extract or a pistachio paste to make it really worthwhile.  I won't remake it.
readable_ai_guess: Didn't like recipe due to flavor
